quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 10 Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: 10.9: Linear Regression and Residuals
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Finding the line of best fit and analyzing prediction errors

questions.question: A linear regression model for wedding cost is $\hat{y} = 150x + 5000$, where $x$ is the number of guests. Predict the cost for 80 guests and identify the fixed cost.
questions.explanation: Slope = $\$150$ per guest. $\hat{y} = 150(80) + 5000 = 12000 + 5000 = 17000$. The y-intercept $\$5{,}000$ is the fixed cost with zero guests.

questions.question: If the actual value of a data point is $y = 25$ and the regression line predicts $\hat{y} = 22$, what is the residual?
A regression line is $\hat{y} = 2x - 1$. Calculate the residuals for the points $(1, 3)$ and $(4, 6)$.
A line of best fit is $\hat{y} = -2x + 10$. If an observed data point is $(3, 2)$, what is the residual?
A regression line predicts $\hat{y} = 4x + 3$. For the data point $(2, 15)$, find the residual and state whether the model under- or overestimated.
questions.explanation: $\hat{y} = 4(2) + 3 = 11$. Residual = $15 - 11 = 4$. Positive residual means actual is above the line — the model predicted too low.

questions.question: A regression line passes through $(2, 5)$ and has slope $3$. Write the equation and find the predicted value at $x = 6$.
A regression line $\hat{y} = x + 2$ is used for points $(1, 5)$, $(2, 3)$, $(3, 6)$. Find the sum of the squared residuals.
questions.explanation: Residuals: $5-3=2$, $3-4=-1$, $6-5=1$. Squared: $4+1+1 = 6$. This is the quantity least squares minimizes.

questions.question: A regression line for height ($y$ in inches) based on age ($x$ in years) is $\hat{y} = 3x + 20$. Predict the height of a 10-year-old.
A regression line $\hat{y} = 5x - 7$ passes through exactly one of these points. Which one?
questions.explanation: Test each: $(2,3)$ → $5(2)-7=3$. Residual $= 0$. Only this point lies exactly on the line.

questions.question: For the regression line $\hat{y} = 2x + 1$, calculate residuals for $(1, 2)$, $(2, 6)$, $(3, 6)$, $(4, 10)$. Do the residuals show a pattern?
questions.explanation: Residuals: $2-3=-1$, $6-5=1$, $6-7=-1$, $10-9=1$. The alternating pattern suggests the data may be non-linear.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can build and analyze regression models — making predictions, calculating residuals, interpreting under/overestimation, finding the sum of squared residuals, and detecting patterns that suggest a non-linear fit.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Key reminders: (1) Residual = Actual − Predicted ($y - \hat{y}$), (2) Positive residual = model underestimated; negative = overestimated, (3) The least squares line minimizes the sum of squared residuals, (4) A pattern in residuals suggests a non-linear model may be better.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Plugging $x$ into the regression equation to get $\hat{y}$, (2) Computing $y - \hat{y}$ for each point, (3) Squaring residuals and summing them, (4) Recognizing that alternating or curved residual patterns indicate a linear model isn't ideal.
scoreMessages.0: Review linear regression: (1) $\hat{y} = mx + b$ is the regression line — plug in $x$ to predict, (2) Residual = $y - \hat{y}$ measures prediction error, (3) Positive residual = actual above line (underestimate), negative = actual below line (overestimate), (4) Sum of squared residuals measures total error.
